title: SPIKE (binding): can a tiny-lane cell run through a worker with the fast path UNCHANGED?
action: Answer ONE question. The answer must be the literal token YES or NO, alone on its own line, under a '## Verdict' heading. THE QUESTION: can a tiny-lane cell execute through a dispatched worker while leaving BOTH of these contract clauses TEXTUALLY UNCHANGED in skills/bee-hive/SKILL.md — (a) the merged Gate 2+3 question at :125, and (b) the Execute column's 'direct, in-session (solo)' at :116 together with the done-report clause (diff + fresh verify output produced BY THE SAME SESSION THAT RAN THE WORK)? YES means one thing only: NO EDIT to :116 or :125 is required. If ANY reconciliation, rewording, reinterpretation, clarification, or 'we are merely restating what it already meant' of either line is needed — including redefining 'in-session' as 'relayed into the session', redefining 'solo', or moving the done-report's authorship from the executing agent to the orchestrator — the answer is NO. There is no third answer: 'yes, with caveats', 'yes if we adjust X', and 'yes under a reconciled contract' are all NO. A NO returns to the USER, with the proposed reconciled wording attached as a QUESTION, never as a resolution — write it under a '## Escalation' heading. A YES ALSO returns to the user for ratification before any cell derived from AO1's tiny clause is planned: a spike verdict does not license a lane-table rewrite. Do not reinterpret AO1 in either direction. Do not implement anything. Anchor every claim about the fast path with file:line.

truths: the spike file contains a '## Verdict' heading followed by a line that is exactly YES or exactly NO
the spike file contains a '## Escalation' section stating precisely what is being handed to the user
every claim about the tiny fast path carries a file:line anchor
no reconciled wording is presented as a resolution — only as a question for the user

artifacts.substantive: question, anchored evidence, a bare YES/NO verdict, and an Escalation section

prohibitions: No source edits — this is a spike; it produces a finding and nothing else
Never reinterpret, narrow, or 'clarify' AO1 or the lane table to make the answer come out YES
Never write a hedged verdict — the Verdict line is one bare token, and any caveat means NO
No cells, no plan changes, no skill edits

verify: test -s .bee/spikes/advisor-and-orchestration/s1-tiny-worker.md && grep -A2 -E '^## Verdict' .bee/spikes/advisor-and-orchestration/s1-tiny-worker.md | grep -qxE '(YES|NO)' && grep -qE '^## Escalation' .bee/spikes/advisor-and-orchestration/s1-tiny-worker.md

trace.outcome: NO. A tiny-lane cell cannot run through a dispatched worker with bee-hive/SKILL.md:116 and :125 textually unchanged. :116's Execute cell reads 'direct, in-session (solo)' — a worker fails all three words independently; the 'I/O-offload workers exempt' parenthetical is scoped by routing-and-contracts.md:195-201 to digest-returning gathers that explicitly do NOT register in the swarm registry ('reservations/status are execution concerns'), so it does not cover an executing worker, and it sits in the Validate column, not Execute. :116/:125's done-report nails authorship to the executing agent ('self-review', 'fresh verify output'). :125's merged Gate 2+3 question alone would survive ('via Y' can name a worker) — but the question demands BOTH lines unchanged. Corroborated by bee-swarming/SKILL.md:22 ('no workers are spawned' for tiny/small) and conceded by CONTEXT.md:99 ('This is what AO1 rewrites'). AO1 and the lane table are in direct contradiction; the choice (amend the table vs amend AO1) escalates to the user as a question, with the reconciled wording proposed as a question and never adopted.
trace.verify_output: EXIT=0 — all three checks passed (file non-empty; the line under '## Verdict' matches ^(YES|NO)$ exactly, it is the bare token NO; '## Escalation' heading present). The greps run with -q, so a pass prints no stdout; exit status 0 is the entire signal. Cross-check that the assertion is real, not vacuous: `grep -A2 -E '^## Verdict' <file>` prints '## Verdict', '', 'NO'.
trace.verify_command: test -s .bee/spikes/advisor-and-orchestration/s1-tiny-worker.md && grep -A2 -E '^## Verdict' .bee/spikes/advisor-and-orchestration/s1-tiny-worker.md | grep -qxE '(YES|NO)' && grep -qE '^## Escalation' .bee/spikes/advisor-and-orchestration/s1-tiny-worker.md
